ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for 'bento set meal'. FOODON_00002473 (meal food product) is the closest defensible parent class. A more specific bento term is not available in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (composite meal / mixed dish) is used as the closest defensible anchor. No specific bento set code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for bento set meal identified in current release. SNOMED CT is not applicable for this composite food record.

main_ingredients: main dish (dumplings, noodles, or ramen — variable)
allergens: gluten (wheat — likely from dumplings or noodles)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bento set meal portion is approximately 650 g, comprising a main dish (dumplings, noodles, or ramen), one hanjuku soft-boiled egg, and pickled sprouts.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Mixed Dishes and Composite Meals Reference Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for a Singaporean bento set meal. Nutrient values are estimated from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central composite meal entries for dumplings (FDC ID 168927), soft-boiled egg (FDC ID 173424), and pickled vegetables. Values are retained from the input as the best available estimate; confidence is set to medium. Singapore HPB Foodo does not carry a specific bento set entry. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients are not reported in the source and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Singaporean bento set meal. GI of 65 is estimated using the analogous food basis: white rice/noodle-dominant mixed meal GI range 60–70 per Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. The bento set is assumed to contain a refined-carbohydrate main dish (white rice, wheat noodles, or dumpling wrappers) consistent with the restaurant description. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 85.02) / 100 ≈ 55.3, rounded to 55.5. Carbohydrate per serving = 13.08 g/100g × 650 g = 85.02 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 276.9 mg/100g; 1,800 mg per full 650 g serving — approaching 78% of the 2,300 mg daily limit in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and unpickled vegetable alternatives, and limit portion size to no more than half a set to keep sodium intake below 900 mg per meal occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated GI 65 (medium) with low dietary fibre 0.77 g/100g; a 650 g serving delivers approximately 85 g of carbohydrate with minimal fibre buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute white rice or refined noodles with brown rice or wholegrain noodles, increase the vegetable proportion to add fibre, and choose a protein-rich main dish to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 276.9 mg/100g, cholesterol 38.46 mg/100g, and saturated fat 1.54 g/100g; full serving delivers approximately 1,800 mg sodium and 250 mg cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal to a half-portion, request steamed or grilled protein instead of pan-fried or pork-based options, and avoid adding extra soy sauce to reduce sodium and saturated fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Unspecified main dish may contain pro-inflammatory pork or processed broth; high sodium from pickled sprouts and soy sauce may worsen tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should request an anti-inflammatory protein main dish (salmon, tofu, or edamame) and reduce soy sauce and pickled sprout quantity to manage sodium-driven inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Bento Set is a complete set meal sold in Singaporean food-delivery and restaurant contexts, typically comprising a variable main dish — most commonly dumplings (pan-fried or steamed), wheat noodles, or ramen — accompanied by a hanjuku soft-boiled egg (marinated in soy sauce and mirin until the yolk is jammy) and pickled sprouts (lightly fermented bean or radish sprouts). The meal draws on Chinese dumpling and noodle traditions as well as Japanese bento-box presentation conventions, reflecting Singapore's multicultural food culture. The total portion is approximately 650 g. Per 100 g, the set delivers 115 kcal, 5.4 g protein, 4.6 g fat, and 13.1 g carbohydrate, with sodium at 276.9 mg — elevated due to soy-based sauces and pickled components. Dietary fibre is low at 0.77 g/100g, which limits glycaemic buffering. The estimated glycaemic index is 65 (medium), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 55 per full serving — clinically significant for Type 2 Diabetes management. Saturated fat is moderate at 1.54 g/100g. The hanjuku egg contributes cholesterol (38.5 mg/100g) and protein. Clinically, the primary concerns are sodium load for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients, and the high carbohydrate-to-fibre ratio for Type 2 Diabetes patients. The meal is not suitable for strict halal or vegetarian diets without modification, as the main dish may contain pork.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a bento set?
Is bento set suitable for diabetics?
What comes in a bento set meal?

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for a Singaporean bento set meal. Nutrient values are retained from the ai_generated input record (confidence 0.7) and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central composite meal entries for the component foods. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables. Confidence is set to medium reflecting the composite estimation basis and variable main-dish composition.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral panel, and moisture data for this composite bento set meal are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. 2. The main dish composition is variable (dumplings, noodles, or ramen); all nutrient values represent a weighted average estimate and should be treated as indicative only. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on refined-carbohydrate mixed meal data from Atkinson et al. (2021); direct GI measurement for this product does not exist. 4. Halal status is unconfirmed as the main dish may contain pork. 5. The input source listed 'USDA FoodData Central — International Cuisine Nutritional Reference' as supporting only 'menu_listing'; nutrient values from that source were not used as primary evidence. Nutrient values are estimated composites cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central component entries.
